Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly states that the full-length cDNA was deposited with accession number AY547352.

Source_Species.reason: The study is on pepper fruits/plants throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and methods identify pepper as Capsicum annuum L.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: qRT-PCR methods specify the cultivar Vergasa used in this work.

Applied_Species.reason: The experimental material used for physiological assays is pepper.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Pepper is identified as Capsicum annuum L. in the materials section.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The paper states that organs used for qRT-PCR were from cultivar Vergasa; this supports the applied variety designation.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The gene is studied in relation to ascorbic acid/ascorbate biosynthesis and fruit ascorbate content.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The abstract and conclusion refer to vitamin C concentration/levels, i.e. ascorbic acid as a vitamin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper describes ascorbate as important for plant physiology and as a vitamin C source for human nutrition/diet.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results Fig. 3C state the highest ascorbate concentrations were detected in fruits, both green and red.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The abstract states that in plants ascorbic acid is mainly synthesized through the L-galactose pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The abstract explicitly identifies GalLDH as the last step of the L-galactose pathway.

Enzyme_Name.reason: GalLDH is explicitly expanded as L-galactono-1,4-lactone dehydrogenase throughout the paper.

EC_Number.reason: Methods section 2.9 explicitly lists GalLDH as EC 1.3.2.3.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results section 3.1 states the pepper GalLDH showed domains for FAD-binding-4, GlcD, and MurB.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper describes a 37-aa mitochondrial targeting signal and one main transmembrane helix; discussion states GalLDH has been located at the mitochondrial inner membrane and their model places it membrane-associated there. This combined wording is supported.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results section 3.2 reports highest transcript levels in leaves and flowers and lowest in fruits and stems; section 3.3 reports strong induction after NO treatment. The phrase 'expression maintained during fruit development/ripening' is consistent with no significant green/red difference and discussion wording about constant expression during development and ripening.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The same pepper cultivar/system shows highest expression in leaves/flowers, lower in fruits/stems, and about 13-fold induction after NO treatment in fruits measured after ripening.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Methods for the activity assay use L-galactono-1,4-lactone as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: GalLDH is presented as the last enzyme in ascorbate biosynthesis, so ascorbic acid is the direct product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper supports that GalLDH catalyzes the final step from L-galactono-1,4-lactone to ascorbate, and discussion states oxidized cytochrome c serves as electron acceptor.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The abstract explicitly states GalLDH is the last/final step of the pathway.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Because GalLDH is the terminal enzyme of the ascorbate pathway, it directly forms the target product.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper repeatedly describes GalLDH as a key player and an important modulation point, while discussion 4.2 explicitly says it is not the only limitation step.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show NO treatment increased fruit ascorbate about 42% alongside ~3-fold GalLDH activity increase and ~13-fold transcript induction, supporting correlation with ascorbate accumulation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is reflected in Results/Abstract: cloning and pathway position, mitochondrial targeting plus main transmembrane helix, activity pattern paralleling ascorbate better than transcript profile, NO-induced transcript/activity/ascorbate increases, and lack of SIN-1/GSNO effect indicating regulation not via nitration/S-nitrosylation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Materials and used in Results: cloning/sequencing, qRT-PCR, enzymatic assay with L-GalL and cytochrome c, immunoblotting, antibody inhibition, ascorbate assays, NO treatment, and SIN-1/GSNO/GSH incubations.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These factors are explicitly described: fruit ripening stage, exogenous NO atmosphere at 5 ppm for 1 h followed by 10 days, and in vitro SIN-1/GSNO/GSH treatments.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Cytochrome c as electron acceptor is directly supported. Association with mitochondrial Complex I is discussed based partly on prior literature and a model, not directly tested in this paper's Results; thus the combined field is only partially supported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show a ~56 kDa immunoreactive band in green/red fruits, activity reduction by antibody, no effect of SIN-1 or GSNO, and ~60% inhibition by GSH; discussion mentions GMP/VTC1 and GGP/VTC2 as other regulatory points not core subjects here.
